economic_notes: Se-mi-ah-mu Limited Partnership carries on commercial property rentals and short-term parking lot rentals. BC First Nation Gaming Revenue Sharing LP received $255,528 in 2020.
notes: Audit is qualified because budget information was not disclosed as required by Canadian public sector accounting standards (Note 12). Consolidated entities include Se-mi-ah-mu Inc. and Se-mi-ah-mu Development Corporation. Se-mi-ah-mu Limited Partnership is accounted for using the modified equity method (GBE). The large surplus of $10M is primarily driven by income from the First Nation business entity ($1.6M) and a significant increase in ISC funding ($9.7M vs $1.2M prior year). Accumulated surplus nearly doubled year over year. Deferred revenue nearly doubled to $4.85M mainly from ISC environmental cleanup and water/wastewater projects. Audit report signed December 4, 2024.
